blocks.content: Gaold walked towards the walls of the Hall of Corruption.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Ever since she heard his desperate cries 20 years ago, she had always felt that Gaold might never give up.
Miro looked at Gaold's muscular body, hidden under the bloodstains.
His once slender frame was now completely different.
His skin was covered in scars, with no space left unmarked.
Anyone could see that his body had been honed through relentless training.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Foolish human…
Miro suppressed the urge to curse out loud.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: You've become quite sexy?
Words she would have easily thrown out before now stayed unspoken.
Perhaps she was a little angry.
Reflecting her feelings, Miro's eyes grew cold.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: He had really come.
He had crushed the fire giants, torn apart countless Maras and fallen angels, and made it here.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: But even if he's here…
What difference does it make?

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Just to have me? To possess me? To hear whispers of love meant only for him while lying beside him?
Any human might desire that, but Gaold had already crossed the line.
This was the heart of Heaven's enemy territory, and with her magic sealed by Kariel, her value was reduced to that of a powerless hostage, weighed against the lives of humanity.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: And…
There were two archangels here.
Kariel and Uriel.
Even if Kariel's power was weakened, teaming up with Uriel, the Archangel of Destruction, made them the worst possible combination.
The conclusion was that even if he came, he couldn't leave.
Gaold would die here, and so would she.
The rest of humanity would be slaughtered by Heaven's army.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Why did you come?
Miro coldly spat out.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: She could control the rising anger, but she couldn't let herself show any affection for Gaold.
Reminding the enemy of the hostage's value would only increase the number of victims.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Because of you, everything is a mess. Do you even know what you've done?
Miro desperately wished.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Go back. Please, even now, go back, you fool!
Seeing Gaold looking up at her in a daze, Miro added some emotion to her words.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: Put it back! You've ruined everything I've dedicated my life to. So put it back…!
Thud!
Gaold's eyes lost focus, and his knees hit the ground.
His body trembled as nerves fired along his spine.
At the end of the terrifying pain was a profound, emotionless calm.
His head, which had been hanging limply, slowly rose again.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: I'm sorry.
Miro clenched her lips, struggling to hide the shockwave exploding in her heart.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It took too long, didn't it? At first…
Gaold's words and breaths tangled as his body trembled again.
The 20 years that had been cut off reconnected, and only his feelings for Miro remained clear.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: At first, I thought it would be quick, but it kept getting delayed. I wanted to come sooner, but I couldn't…
As Gaold's eyes reddened, Miro's eyes also filled with warmth.
The emotions condensed to the extreme in one human couldn't be expressed in mere words.
Even without words, the fact that Gaold had come this far spoke volumes about his 20-year journey.
Realizing this, Gaold stopped explaining and looked up.
His eyes, which had been half-conscious, finally returned to normal.
No one knew, but Miro recognized this as the Gaold from 20 years ago.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Let's go back, Miro. Let's just… go back.
Miro gritted her teeth.
She couldn't even identify the emotions surging within her.
Anger, resentment, or perhaps something else she wanted to disguise as those feelings.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: I must die.
Her rational mind immediately offered a solution to block her emotions.
She had to die here.
Even though the situation was dire, if there was even a slight chance to prevent the final war, some of those dispatched to Heaven had to survive and leave Lakia.

blocks.speaker: Miro
blocks.content: I…
As Miro steeled herself and began to speak, a massive flash of light erupted from the rear of the Hall of Corruption.
The immense energy wave traveled dozens of kilometers to reach them, causing Kariel and Uriel to turn their heads.
BOOM
The shockwave was coming from Araboth, where Anke Ra was.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: Ikael…
Kariel muttered with a twisted expression.
Currently, the archangels who governed the four fundamental forces were testing Ikael's qualifications as the archangel.
If the archangels of the four forces subdued Ikael, it would be beneficial for him.
They would act according to their own judgments outside the archangel's authority, and his decisions would align with theirs, making them less significant.
On the other hand, if Ikael subdued the archangels of the four forces, the situation would become complicated.
She wouldn't tolerate the angels' reckless behavior, and Kariel, having lost the fire giants, would also be threatened.
Therefore, he had to hope that the archangels of the four forces would defeat Ikael, but Kariel found himself unable to choose.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: A part of him couldn't accept the idea of Ikael losing to anyone.
Kariel shook his head.
She had to be defeated.
But the one to do it had to be him, and no one else.

blocks.speaker: Kariel
blocks.content: I will crush her. Only I have the right to crush her.
BOOM
An even stronger energy wave swept through Lakia.
The high spire of Araboth shook from deep within the ground.
Boom!
The area where the wave passed was left spotless.
It was the epitome of purity, unseen in the human world.
Huff, huff.
Ikael knelt on one knee, breathing heavily.
Around her, the archangels lay as if dead.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: She's strong.
The thought simultaneously crossed the minds of the archangels of the four forces.
Indeed, the supreme law of Heaven, Ataraxia.
Her amplification was unique, beyond the reach of intellect, a concept before existence and non-existence.
The archangels of the four forces were each powerful in their own right, but even with all four of them combined, they could only bring her to a groggy state.

blocks.speaker: Metatron
blocks.content: Phew, let's consider this test concluded.
Metatron was the first to rise.

blocks.speaker: Metatron
blocks.content: For now, Ikael, you are still the archangel. We trust you and leave the judgment regarding Shirone entirely to you.
Shirone.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Ikael thought of that name as she slowly stood up.
Perhaps she could have persuaded them with words alone.
But she had defended her judgment regarding Shirone, even if it meant a show of force among angels.
Why?
What was that boy to her?

blocks.speaker: Metatron
blocks.content: We agree with Metatron's opinion.
But as archangels governing Heaven, we cannot overlook human recklessness.
We will not engage in the war by Anke Ra's command, but we will intervene through our Maras.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Anke Ra's command was only to prohibit angelic activities.
As Ikael indirectly expressed her agreement and emphasized once more, the archangels of the four forces nodded briefly and looked at each other.
The battles were taking place in all six heavens except Araboth.
Each would oversee one and handle it.

blocks.speaker: Ikael
blocks.content: Then, let's go.
As the four archangels simultaneously spread their wings of light, a sacred vibration created a harmony that shook the world.
Watching the archangels disappear in an instant, Ikael turned coldly and headed for the spire of Araboth.
The only one she had to protect was Anke Ra.
Thud.
Uriel moved for the first time.
As he jumped down from the wall and landed on the ground, the entire scenery seemed to bend towards him.
As he took a step forward, Gaold stepped back at the same pace.
Having never retreated before, Gaold's followers, including Sein, could guess the pressure he was under.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: So this is how it ends.
Sein controlled his emotions.
They had made it this far, but the true challenge wasn't the fire giants, the Maras, or the fallen angels.
It was the archangel standing before them.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: Gaold.
I'll handle this.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Rescue Miro in the meantime.
Sein knew it was an impossible plan.
Even if all ten of them attacked Uriel, it was doubtful how long they could hold out, let alone Gaold facing him alone.

blocks.speaker: Sein
blocks.content: But we have to try.
As everyone, led by Sein, steeled themselves, a powerful aura exploded from Uriel's body.
Pure destruction.
The aura was so overwhelming that any being, no, any existence, buried in it would be utterly destroyed, leaving no bones behind.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: No one can pass me. The only thing you can do here is die.
Gaold spoke.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Go.
Zulu moved first, followed by the others.
They might be annihilated upon approach, but without trying, there was no way to save Miro.
The seven members scattered left and right, passing Uriel.

blocks.speaker: Kangnan
blocks.content: What is this?
Without any time to gather his thoughts, Kangnan broke through the castle gate with a powerful stomp and charged inside.
Just before entering the Hall of Corruption, Sain turned around.
Uriel was still staring solely at Gaold.

blocks.speaker: Sain
blocks.content: Was he testing us?
Though he clenched his teeth in frustration at the sense of mockery, missing such an opportunity would have been foolish.
Sain led everyone in their infiltration of the Hall of Corruption.
Gaold slowly curled his lips into a grin.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Are you offering mercy, befitting a great archangel?
Uriel gave no reply.
Even knowing they will die, they charge forward. That is humanity.
And among all humans, the one standing before Uriel now—Gaold—gave off the most bizarre impression.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hope?
A concentrated sphere of atmospheric pressure formed in Gaold's right hand.
It was the Vacuum Press, powerful enough to block even the Photon Cannon of Ataraxia.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: Hope is nothing more than an illusion born of despair. What drives humanity isn't hope. It's something far more brutal…
Gaold's body shot forward like an arrow.
In an instant, he reached Uriel's flank and swung his right hand, unleashing the Vacuum Press.

blocks.speaker: Gaold
blocks.content: It's despair.
